news.title: 美国教育部就涉嫌反犹太歧视对纽约市教育局启动《民权法案》第六条调查
news.content:
April 23, 2026
Today, the U.S. Department of Education’s Office for Civil Rights (OCR) opened an investigation into the New York City Department of Education (NYCDOE) to determine whether NYCDOE violated Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI) by discriminating against Jewish students.
Many reports received by OCR indicate that a group of NYCDOE employees organized a series of teaching seminars focused on “Palestine, Zionism, and Resistance.” The so-called “NYC Educators for Palestine” allegedly teach children as young as five about “contemporary and historical Palestinian resistance,” that Zionists are “genocidal white supremacists,” and to support the federally designated terrorist organization Hamas and its “martyrs” (i.e. dead terrorists). Complaints received by OCR allege that these actions in NYCDOE teach and sow hostility and hatred towards Jewish students, potentially creating a hostile environment.
“No child should be taught by his or her teachers to hate their peers. Neither should Jewish children be taught that being Jewish somehow makes them inherently guilty or proponents of hate and violence,”
said Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights Kimberly Richey.
“Discrimination has no place in our schools, and, unlike the previous Administration, the Trump Administration will not turn a blind eye to antisemitic harassment. OCR will investigate these appalling allegations to ensure the equal treatment of all students.”
Background
On January 29, 2025, President Trump signed an Executive Order titled
Additional Measures to Combat Anti-Semitism,
stating that it is “the policy of the United States to combat anti-Semitism vigorously, using all available and appropriate legal tools.”
Title VI and its implementing regulations prohibit discrimination on the basis of race, color, and national origin in educational programs and activities receiving federal financial assistance.
Contact
Press Office
(202) 401-1576
press@ed.gov
Tags
Civil Rights
Title VI
news.original_title: U.S. Department of Education Initiates Title VI Investigation into NYC Department of Education for Alleged Antisemitic Discrimination
news.summary_cn:
2026年4月23日，美国教育部民权办公室宣布对纽约市教育局启动调查，核实其是否违反1964年《民权法案》第六条，存在歧视犹太学生的行为。

民权办公室收到的多份报告显示，纽约市教育局部分员工组建的"纽约市巴勒斯坦教育工作者"团体举办了一系列以"巴勒斯坦、犹太复国主义与抵抗"为主题的教学研讨会，面向低至5岁的儿童讲授巴勒斯坦当代及历史上的"抵抗"相关内容，称犹太复国主义者是"实施种族灭绝的白人至上主义者"，教唆学生支持被美国联邦政府认定为恐怖组织的哈马斯及其"殉道者"。相关投诉指出，上述行为向学生灌输对犹太学生的敌意与仇恨，可能营造敌对校园环境。

美国教育部民权事务助理部长金伯利·里奇表示，任何儿童都不应被教师灌输仇恨同辈的观念，犹太儿童也不应被告知其犹太人身份意味着天然有罪、是仇恨与暴力的支持者。特朗普政府不会对反犹太骚扰视而不见，本次调查将核实相关指控，保障所有学生获得平等对待。2025年1月29日，美国总统特朗普签署《打击反犹太主义额外措施》行政令，要求动用一切适当合法工具大力打击反犹太主义；《民权法案》第六条及其实施条例禁止接受联邦财政资助的教育项目及活动基于种族、肤色、原国籍实施歧视。

news.title: 美国教育部高等教育事务助理部长大卫·巴克博士在华盛顿“历史闪耀！”活动中强调公民教育
news.content:
April 23, 2026
LIBERTY LAKE, WA —
Yesterday, U.S. Department of Education Assistant Secretary for Postsecondary Education Dr. David Barker visited Liberty Launch Academy as part of the Department of Education’s national
History Rocks!
Trail to Independence Tour
, in celebration of America’s upcoming 250th birthday. The Tour aims to strengthen civic literacy by helping students personally connect with America’s founding principles.
Students in kindergarten through twelfth grades participated in interactive games designed to deepen their understanding of American history and civics and the important role Washington has played in our nation’s history. The event opened with remarks from Founder and Chairman Luke Kjar, followed by Assistant Secretary Barker. Students explored themes of civic engagement, patriotism, and the shared responsibilities of citizenship.
“Washington has always been a place where the edge of the map meant opportunity, not uncertainty. From early expeditions to the industries shaping the future today, there is a throughline of people willing to try something new and see where it leads,”
said
U.S. Department of Education Assistant Secretary for Postsecondary Education Dr. David Barker
. “
History Rocks!
gives students a way to connect that mindset to the American story, not as something distant, but as something they are still writing themselves.”
Background
The
History Rocks!
initiative is a key component of the U.S. Department of Education’s celebrations for America's 250th birthday, in partnership with Freedom 250 and coordinated with the America 250 Civics Education Coalition. Secretary McMahon, Administration officials, and education leaders will visit schools in all 50 states to showcase examples of high-quality history and civics instruction that bring American principles to life.
Learn more about the Department’s Semiquincentennial initiatives
here
.
Freedom 250 leads the presidential programming for the nation’s 250th anniversary celebration. Its mission is to unite Americans around one shared purpose—celebrating the Triumph of the American Spirit by honoring our history, cherishing our freedoms, and building the golden age of opportunity for the next 250 years.
Learn more about Freedom 250
here
.
The America 250 Civics Education Coalition is a national partnership with the America First Policy Institute, Turning Point USA, Hillsdale College, and more than 50 national and state organizations. This coalition is dedicated to advancing civic education and expanding opportunities for students to learn about the ideas, individuals, and events that define the American story.
Contact
Press Office
(202) 401-1576
press@ed.gov
Tags
America250
news.original_title: U.S. Department of Education Assistant Secretary for Postsecondary Education Dr. David Barker Highlights Civics Education at “History Rocks!” Event in Washington
news.summary_cn:
2026年4月23日，美国教育部官方发布公务新闻，通报该部高级官员出席教育主题公共活动的相关情况。

本次通报涉及的活动为在华盛顿举办的“历史闪耀！”（History Rocks!）主题活动，美国教育部高等教育事务助理部长大卫·巴克博士出席本次活动，并在活动环节重点就公民教育相关内容作出表态。

本次公开的新闻信息暂未披露活动的具体举办主体、参与人员范围、大卫·巴克博士关于公民教育表态的详细内容、相关后续工作安排等更多细节，有关后续信息以美国教育部官方发布渠道的通报为准。
